topic: Since / For (Time Expressions with Perfect Tenses)
keyPointsSummary: 'For' is used with a duration/period of time (for two years, for a week)
'Since' is used with a starting point in time (since 2020, since Monday)
Both are commonly used with perfect and perfect continuous tenses

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं दो साल से यहाँ रह रहा हूँ।
quickQuiz.answer: I have been living here for two years.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं 2020 से यहाँ रह रहा हूँ।
quickQuiz.answer: I have been living here since 2020.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह तीन घंटे से पढ़ रही है।
quickQuiz.answer: She has been studying for three hours.

quickQuiz.hindi: वह सुबह से पढ़ रही है।
quickQuiz.answer: She has been studying since morning.

quickQuiz.hindi: हम पाँच साल से दोस्त हैं।
quickQuiz.answer: We have been friends for five years.

quickQuiz.hindi: हम कॉलेज के समय से दोस्त हैं।
quickQuiz.answer: We have been friends since college.

quickQuiz.hindi: उसने एक हफ्ते से यह काम नहीं किया।
quickQuiz.answer: He has not done this work for a week.

quickQuiz.hindi: उसने सोमवार से यह काम नहीं किया।
quickQuiz.answer: He has not done this work since Monday.

quickQuiz.hindi: मुझे दस साल से यह जानकारी है।
quickQuiz.answer: I have known this for ten years.

mustRememberRules: 'For' is used with a duration/period of time (for two years, for a week)
'Since' is used with a starting point in time (since 2020, since Monday)
Both are commonly used with perfect and perfect continuous tenses
